title: Repository Pair API Routes
content:
This file defines the API routes for managing repository pairs within the DocMed application.

## Purpose

This API handles the creation and retrieval of repository pairs, which likely represent linked repositories used for documentation generation or comparison.

## Key Exports/Functions

*   **`GET /api/repo-pairs`**: Retrieves a list of all repository pairs associated with the authenticated user.
    *   Requires user authentication. Unauthorized requests will receive a 401.
    *   Returns a JSON array of repository pair objects.
*   **`POST /api/repo-pairs`**: Creates a new repository pair for the authenticated user.
    *   Requires user authentication. Unauthorized requests will receive a 401.
    *   Accepts a JSON request body with an optional `name` property.
    *   If `name` is not provided, a default name like `Pair N` (where N is the next available number) will be generated.
    *   Returns a JSON object containing the `id` of the newly created repository pair with a 201 status code.

## Dependencies

*   `next/server` for request and response handling.
*   `@clerk/nextjs/server` for authentication.
*   `@/lib/repo-pairs` for core repository pair logic (creation and listing).

## Usage Example (POST request)

```bash
curl -X POST https://your-domain.com/api/repo-pairs \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"name": "My Project Docs"}'
```

This would create a new repository pair named "My Project Docs" for the authenticated user.
